items.text:
Quigley, this way of getting around
is ... cute. I don't mind it.

Of course I have always been a fan
of the binary partitioning

we use for National Transport
(confusing as it may be to newcomers);

items.comment: "enables travel only along linear routes" is kind of word salad; @Revise.
items.text:
but the weakness of our scheme is that it
enables travel only along linear routes.

With the method on display here, travel is
two-dimensional and free.

So. How do these transportation platforms work?
Could they exist in our world too,

or is there something
about the fabric of this place
absent in ours?
